cards.title: Common pitfalls when building generative AI applications
cards.desc: <p>As we’re still in the early days of building applications with foundation models, it’s normal to make mistakes. This is a quick note with examples of some of the most common pitfalls that I’ve seen, both from public case studies and from my personal experience.</p> <p>Because th
cards.author: Chip Huyen

cards.title: Agents
cards.desc: <p>Intelligent agents are considered by many to be the ultimate goal of AI. The classic book by Stuart Russell and Peter Norvig, <em>Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach</em> (Prentice Hall, 1995), defines the field of AI research as “<em>the study and design of rationa
cards.author: Chip Huyen

cards.title: Building A Generative AI Platform
cards.desc: <p>After studying how companies deploy generative AI applications, I noticed many similarities in their platforms. This post outlines the common components of a generative AI platform, what they do, and how they are implemented. I try my best to keep the architecture general, but certain appli
cards.author: Chip Huyen

cards.title: Measuring personal growth
cards.desc: <p>My founder friends constantly think about growth. They think about how to measure their business growth and how to get to the next order of magnitude scale. If they’re making $1M ARR today, they think about how to get to $10M ARR. If they have 1,000 users today, they think about how to get
cards.author: Chip Huyen

cards.title: NirDiamant/Agent_Memory_Techniques
cards.desc: Agent memory for LLMs: 30 runnable Jupyter notebooks covering conversation buffers, vector stores, knowledge graphs, episodic and semantic memory, MemGPT, Mem0, Letta, Zep, Graphiti, LoCoMo benchmarks, and production patterns.

cards.title: Predictive Human Preference: From Model Ranking to Model Routing
cards.desc: <p>A challenge of building AI applications is choosing which model to use. What if we don’t have to? What if we can predict the best model for any prompt? Predictive human preference aims to predict which model users might prefer for a specific query.</p> <p>Human preference has em
cards.author: Chip Huyen

cards.title: Generation configurations: temperature, top-k, top-p, and test time compute
cards.desc: <p>ML models are probabilistic. Imagine that you want to know what’s the best cuisine in the world. If you ask someone this question twice, a minute apart, their answers both times should be the same. If you ask a model the same question twice, its answer can change. If the model thinks that V
cards.author: Chip Huyen

cards.title: Multimodality and Large Multimodal Models (LMMs)
cards.desc: <p>For a long time, each ML model operated in one data mode – text (translation, language modeling), image (object detection, image classification), or audio (speech recognition).</p> <p>However, natural intelligence is not limited to just a single modality. Humans can read, talk,
cards.author: Chip Huyen

cards.author: Taranjeet Slatch

cards.title: Emmimal/control-layer
cards.desc: A production-grade control layer that sits between your application logic and any LLM — input validation, schema enforcement, circuit breaking, targeted retry, and audit logging in one composable pipeline.
cards.author: Emmimal

AI Stock Market Crash fears exploded across Wall Street on June 5 after one of the most brutal selloffs in technology stocks since the AI revolution began. The Nasdaq plunged 4.2%, losing more than 1,100 points in a single session. Semiconductor companies colle
cards.author: Many Mcglon

cards.title: ather-techie/rag-interview-system
cards.desc: A complete collection of RAG interview questions, answers (204 questions & 12 RAG types), system design scenarios, architecture patterns, and production-ready concepts.
cards.author: ather-techie

cards.title: IrtezaAsadRizvi/ai-megalist
cards.desc: Curated index of 200+ AI tools, one writeup per tool with hands-on takes. Covers coding, design, research, video, voice, agents, music, local LLMs. Compare alternatives, see real pricing, find the right tool for the job.
cards.author: IrtezaAsadRizvi

cards.title: MonetiseBG/circuit-breaker
cards.desc: Runtime economic governance for AI agents. Circuit Breaker adds risk-managed execution, cost ceilings, and kill switches to autonomous AI workflows.
cards.author: MonetiseBG

cards.title: crimeacs/auto-improve
cards.desc: GAN-style self-improvement loop for any text artifact: mutate, grade with a SEPARATE model, keep only verified wins (pairwise-judged), revert the rest. The git history is the improvement log.
cards.author: crimeacs

cards.title: Emmimal/llm-eval-layer
cards.desc: A lightweight LLM evaluation layer that turns subjective model outputs into deterministic decisions by scoring attribution, specificity, relevance, and context quality—designed to detect hallucinations and enable production-grade LLM reliability.
cards.author: Emmimal
